Fertilizing cilantro is beneficial because herbs are heavy feeders. A general-purpose fertilizer might not be ideal, as it is often formulated for flowers, shrubs, or vegetables with different nutrient needs.
A balanced, water-soluble fertilizer specifically formulated for herbs (10-10-10 NPK) or a high-phosphorus/blooming formula can promote healthy leaf growth and flavor in cilantro.

When fertilizing cilantro, start with a weak solution (half the recommended strength) applied once a week. Fertilizers specifically designed for herbs might contain additional micronutrients promoting flavor and aroma.

Basil loves nutrients, especially nitrogen, to promote leaf growth and flavor. If you're harvesting your basil regularly, it might benefit from a balanced fertilizer (10-10-10 NPK) to replace the nutrients you're removing. You can fertilize your basil every 1-2 weeks, but start with a weak solution (half the recommended strength) to avoid burning the roots.

Mint is a hardy, fast-growing herb that can thrive in a variety of conditions. While it might not need fertilization as urgently as basil, a balanced fertilizer can still promote healthy growth and flavor. You can fertilize your mint every 2-3 weeks, but be cautious not to overfertilize, as this can lead to weak growth and a decrease in flavor.

When fertilizing your basil and mint, make sure to follow the instructions on the fertilizer packaging and start with a weak solution. You can always increase the strength if needed, but it's harder to correct overfertilization.

If you decide to fertilize your basil and mint, you can use the same herb-specific fertilizer you'll be using for your cilantro. Just remember to adjust the frequency and strength based on each plant's needs.

He used light and shadows to create a sense of depth and atmosphere, depicting the natural world realistically and adding a sense of drama and emotion to his works, which creates a sense of movement and energy that draws the viewer into the scene.

The Eiffel Tower was designed specifically for a World's Fair. There was a mixed reaction to the Eiffel Tower when it was first unveiled. Some people were impressed by the engineering feat and the iconic design of the tower, while others were critical of its appearance and the fact that it dominated the Paris skyline. Some French artists and intellectuals even signed a petition in protest of the tower, calling it a "giant black smokestack" that was a blight on the city's beauty.

The Eiffel Tower was initially designed to be a temporary structure for the World's Fair and was only supposed to stand for 20 years. However, it was so popular that it remained standing beyond its original intended lifespan and was eventually repurposed as a radio tower. Today, it serves as a major tourist attraction, and its once-controversial design has become a beloved symbol of French culture and engineering excellence.
The Eiffel Tower, originally intended as a temporary structure for the 1889 Exposition, became a permanent landmark of the Paris skyline.
Many tourists visit the Eiffel Tower annually, making it one of the most popular attractions in Paris. Visitors can reach the top via stairs or elevators, enjoying panoramic views of the city.
The Eiffel Tower features several observation decks, restaurants, and a glass floor allowing visitors to look down at the ground beneath their feet.

The Eiffel Tower has several elevators that can take visitors from the ground level to the upper platform. The fastest elevator takes only 40 seconds to reach the top of the tower, which is located 324 meters above the ground. However, this elevator may not always be available, depending on the number of visitors and the time of the day.

Travel experts recommend spring (April to May) and early summer (June) for river cruises in Europe due to milder weather, blooming flora, lower prices, and fewer crowds. Temperatures range from 10 to 20°C (50-68°F) with occasional rainfalls.

July to August is considered the high season for river cruises in Europe, characterized by warmer weather, longer days, numerous events and attractions in major cities, high humidity, large crowds, and higher prices. Temperatures are around 25°C (77°F).

Fall (September to October) is known as the harvest season for river cruises in Europe, offering stunning foliage, crisp air, wine festivals, gastronomic tours, fewer tourists, and lower prices. Temperatures range from 5 to 15°C (41-59°F).

Winter (November to March) offers fewer river cruises in Europe but can be appealing for budget travelers interested in experiencing Christmas markets, snow-covered landscapes, and cultural events. Temperatures range from -3 to 10°C (27-50°F).

If the data fed into machine learning models is of poor quality, it is highly likely that the predictions generated will also be inaccurate. Poor quality data can result in biased model outcomes, leading to erroneous predictions and making the results unreliable.

A lack of attention to data quality can lead to missed opportunities for advanced analytics. Machine learning models require significant amounts of high-quality data to work effectively, and poor data quality can lead to missing out on valuable insights.
Poor data quality can also result in increased risk of errors and inconsistencies. These errors can have a cascading effect, resulting in poor decision-making that can impact business operations.

Data quality issues can reduce confidence in the results provided by machine learning models. This can be a significant challenge for data-driven decision making, especially if stakeholders do not trust the results produced by the model.

Skipping the data cleaning process can lead to inaccurate and unreliable results, and the predictions or insights obtained from machine learning algorithms may not be accurate. Data quality is critical for the success of any advanced analytics techniques, including machine learning. Poor data quality can cause biases, errors, and inconsistencies in the model's outcomes.

While data cleaning can be a time-consuming process, skipping it can result in costly errors further down the line. Automated data cleaning tools can be used to speed up the process, and the effort involved in cleaning and preparing data can be minimized by adopting best practices for data management, quality control, and data governance.
Data cleaning is a foundational step in any data analytics workflow, including machine learning applications. It needs to be performed continuously to maintain data quality over time.

Hiring more data scientists to clean data may not be practical or cost-effective, especially for organizations with large datasets. Automation can help make data cleaning more manageable and affordable, improving efficiency and speed while ensuring consistent results.

Training machine learning models on low-quality data can still generate insights, but these insights may not be accurate, reliable, or useful. Poor quality data can lead to inaccurate predictions or potential biases in the model outcomes, which may hinder decision-making or waste valuable resources.

The quality of the data used to train machine learning models is critical to the quality of the predictions and insights generated by the model. Poor-quality data can provide misleading insights and results, rendering the model ineffective.

Poor-quality data can introduce inaccuracies and biases into analytics models and decisions. These inaccuracies can be difficult to identify, leading to poor business outcomes, missed opportunities, or even legal and regulatory issues.

By investing in data quality measures, data-driven organizations can gain a competitive advantage by making better decisions, improving efficiency, and reducing costs. High-quality data can also enable advanced analytics techniques like machine learning to deliver accurate and actionable insights that drive better business outcomes.

Healthy Chicken Salad Recipe makes about 6 servings, using 1 1/2 pounds boneless, skinless chicken breast, cooked and diced, 1/2 cup plain Greek yogurt, 1/4 cup chopped celery, 1/4 cup chopped red bell pepper, 1/4 cup chopped cucumber, 1/4 cup chopped walnuts, 2 tablespoons Dijon mustard, 1 tablespoon honey, salt and pepper to taste, and optional fresh parsley or dill.

To prepare the Healthy Chicken Salad, mix chicken, Greek yogurt, celery, bell pepper, cucumber, and walnuts in a large bowl. Whisk Dijon mustard and honey in a small bowl until smooth, then add to the chicken mixture and stir until combined. Season with salt and pepper, refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, and garnish with fresh parsley or dill before serving.

For bulk preparation, divide the Healthy Chicken Salad into 6 portions (about 1/2 cup each) and store in airtight containers in the refrigerator for up to 5 days. Use it as a topping for salads, wraps, sandwiches, or serve with crackers or veggies. Double the batch and freeze some for future meals.

Variations of the Healthy Chicken Salad include adding diced jalapeño or serrano peppers for extra heat, incorporating chopped spinach, kale, or arugula for more nutrition, or substituting almonds, pecans, or pistachios for walnuts.

To make homemade granola bars, preheat the oven to 325°F (160°C), line an 8x8 inch baking dish with parchment paper, mix 2 cups rolled oats, 1 cup chopped nuts, and 1/2 cup dried fruits in a bowl, mix 1/2 cup honey, 1/4 cup nut butter, and 1/4 cup chia seeds in another bowl, combine both mixtures, press into the dish, bake for 20-25 minutes until golden, let cool, then cut into bars.

The nutrition information for the homemade granola bar recipe (1 bar, approximately 1 inch x 4 inches) includes: 220 calories, 4g protein, 8g fat, 1g saturated fat, 30g carbohydrates, 4g fiber, 8g sugar, and 50mg sodium. The macronutrient breakdown is 55% carbohydrates, 15% protein, and 30% fat.
For the Cranberry Orange variation, add 1/2 cup dried cranberries and 1 tablespoon orange zest to the mixture, and use orange extract instead of vanilla extract.
In the Peanut Butter Banana variation, add 1/2 cup mashed banana and 2 tablespoons peanut butter to the mixture, and use peanut butter instead of nut butter.
For the Chocolate Chip variation, add 1/2 cup dark chocolate chips to the mixture, and use cocoa powder instead of nuts for a chocolate-flavored granola.

The Pumpkin Spice variation involves adding 1/2 cup canned pumpkin puree, 1 teaspoon pumpkin pie spice, and 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon to the mixture, and using nutmeg instead of cinnamon for extra flavor.
In the Nutty Coconut variation, add 1/2 cup shredded coconut and 1/4 cup chopped macadamia nuts to the mixture, and use coconut oil instead of nut butter for extra coconut flavor.
For the Cinnamon Raisin variation, add 1/2 cup raisins and 1 teaspoon cinnamon to the mixture, and use cinnamon instead of vanilla extract for extra flavor.
The Mocha variation requires adding 1/2 cup instant coffee powder and 1/4 cup chocolate chips to the mixture, and using coffee extract instead of vanilla extract.

Tips for creating unique granola bar flavors include experimenting with different spices, nuts, and seeds; using various nut butters like almond butter or cashew butter; adding a sprinkle of sea salt or cinnamon; and trying different sweeteners such as honey or maple syrup.

The Cranberry Orange Granola Bar recipe requires the following ingredients: rolled oats, nut butter (such as peanut butter, almond butter, or cashew butter), honey, chia seeds, vanilla extract, salt, dried cranberries (1/2 cup), chopped nuts (such as almonds, walnuts, or pecans) (1 cup), orange zest (1 tablespoon), cinnamon (optional), parchment paper, and an 8x8 inch baking dish. Optional additions include orange extract for a stronger orange flavor.

In the first century C.E., much of Europe, North Africa, and the Middle East were under the rule of the Roman Empire. The Roman Empire was a vast and powerful state that controlled a large portion of the western world at that time.

The Roman Empire was founded in 27 BC and lasted until the fall of the Western Roman Empire in 476 AD. During the first century AD, the Roman Empire was at the height of its power and influence, and its territories stretched from modern-day England and Wales in the north, to Egypt in the south, and as far east as present-day Turkey.

Despite the harsh conditions in winter, the snow-capped mountains and winter landscapes around Sumela Monastery can make for a beautiful visit.
Sumela Monastery is located in the Macka district of Trabzon province in Turkey. Trabzon city is the nearest major city and is around an hour away by car.

From Trabzon, the easiest way to get to Sumela Monastery is by car, either renting one or taking a taxi. Minibuses or dolmus from the central city bus station to Macka, a town close to Sumela Monastery, are also available. From Macka, taxis or minibuses can be hired to reach the monastery.
Sumela Monastery is around a 2-hour drive from Rize. Travel options include renting a car, taking a taxi, or taking a bus from central Rize to Macka, followed by a minibus or taxi to the monastery.

Sumela Monastery is about a 3-hour drive from Giresun. Options include renting a car, taking a taxi, or taking buses that run between Giresun and Trabzon, although the journey may take slightly longer.
DoubleTree by Hilton Trabzon is located in Trabzon city, about an hour's drive from Sumela Monastery. It offers comfortable rooms, traditional Turkish cuisine, and an indoor pool.

Sumela Garden Hotel is a cozy boutique hotel located in Macka, just minutes away from Sumela Monastery by car. It offers rooms with mountain views, a restaurant serving local cuisine, and a garden for guests to relax in.

Sisigu Hotel is located in Trabzon city, and it is about an hour's drive from Sumela Monastery. It offers simple yet comfortable rooms with sea views, a restaurant serving local cuisine, and a terrace with panoramic views.

Hayati Pansiyon is a guesthouse located in Sumela village, just a short walk from Sumela Monastery. It offers rooms with views of the mountains, a restaurant serving local cuisine, and a garden for guests to relax in.
Ayder Plateau is an alpine plateau located in the Rize province of Turkey, known for its natural hot springs, terrace farms, and scenic landscapes.
Uzungol Lake is a picturesque lake located in the Trabzon province, surrounded by dense forests and mountains. It is a popular spot for fishing, boating, and hiking.
Firtina Valley is a deep and narrow valley located in the Rize province, renowned for its lush greenery, rushing waterfalls, and traditional wooden houses.
Zigana Mountains are a stunning range of mountains located in the Gümüshane province of Turkey, providing excellent trekking and hiking opportunities with breathtaking views.
Karadeniz Coastal Highway is a scenic coastal drive located along the Black Sea coast in the Pontus region, offering beautiful views of the sea and surrounding mountains.

Facebook has faced significant criticism over the past few years for not doing enough to protect user data. The company has implemented stricter data controls, allowing users greater control over the personal information they provide to the site.
Facebook has limited access to user data for third-party apps, specifically limiting the information shared after the Cambridge Analytica scandal.
Facebook has hired more staff to monitor user data and ensure that it is adequately protected.
Facebook has started publishing quarterly transparency reports, which provide details on government requests for user data.
Facebook has reviewed and updated its policies to ensure that personal data is adequately protected.
Facebook has paid hefty fines for not complying with data protection and privacy laws.
The company has conducted third-party audits of its data protection processes to help identify potential areas of risk.

Facebook faced significant scrutiny and criticism following the Cambridge Analytica scandal, where personal data of millions of Facebook users were harvested without their consent. This incident led to increased concerns about Facebook's handling of user data and privacy.

Facebook experienced several high-profile data breaches, including one where attackers exploited vulnerabilities to steal access tokens and gain unauthorized access to millions of users' accounts. These breaches raised significant concerns about Facebook's commitment to safeguarding user data.

In response to these issues, Facebook invested heavily in security and privacy measures, made product changes, and implemented stricter data controls. The company allowed users greater control over their personal information and limited third-party app access to user data.

Sharing a lot of personal information online has made individuals more vulnerable to data breaches and cyber attacks. Over the years, the number of data breaches has increased, involving major companies where people trusted their personal information to be safe.
Facebook collects a lot of data about its users, including their personal information, likes, and interests, which it uses to target them with ads.
Other popular social media platforms like Twitter, Instagram, and LinkedIn also collect user data to some extent.

Many online services and apps collect a significant amount of user data like search and browsing history, location data, and more. This data is used to provide personalized services and to target ads to users.
Data collection is essential for many online services to function, but it's important to be aware of the information being collected and how it is being used.
To protect personal data, it's essential to review the privacy policies of online services and apps you use.
Being cautious about the information shared online and using privacy settings to limit visible information can help protect personal data.

semantic_nodes.semantic_memory: "The Immortalists" by Chloe Benjamin follows four siblings who visit a fortune teller predicting their exact dates of death as children, exploring love, loss, and the human experience with a similar depth and emotional resonance.

Chloe Benjamin's novel "The Immortalists" explores the lives of four siblings—Varya, Daniel, Klara, and Simon—who visit a fortune teller in New York City's Lower East Side as children and are given the exact dates of their deaths. The novel traces their lives over several decades as they grow up, fall in love, and navigate their careers while carrying the weight of their predicted mortality.
tags: New York City's Lower East Side

semantic_nodes.semantic_memory: Benjamin handles the theme of mortality with sensitivity and depth, exploring difficult questions and emotions without sensationalizing the concept. The prophecy serves as a catalyst for the characters to explore how they live their lives, make choices, and prioritize their time.

Varya, the eldest sibling, becomes obsessed with science and longevity, dedicating her life to understanding and defying aging. Her quest for knowledge and control becomes an all-consuming force in her life.
Daniel, the charmer, uses his prediction as an excuse to live life to the fullest, taking risks and pursuing pleasure without regard for consequences.
Klara, the free spirit, becomes an artist, using her creativity as a way to transcend the limitations of her predicted fate.
Simon, the youngest sibling, struggles to come to terms with his mortality, grappling with anxiety and feelings of powerlessness.

The novel explores themes such as the human desire for control and agency in the face of uncertainty, the impact of trauma and fear on personal relationships and decision-making, the tension between living in the present and preparing for the future, the search for meaning and purpose in life, and the complexities of sibling relationships and family dynamics.

The writing in "The Immortalists" is lyrical, evocative, and introspective, with a strong focus on character development. The novel's pace is measured, allowing the reader to absorb the characters' experiences and emotions.
Despite the heavy subject matter, the tone of "The Immortalists" is ultimately hopeful and thought-provoking, encouraging readers to reflect on their own lives and priorities.

semantic_nodes.semantic_memory: In 2006, Gretchen Rubin, a self-proclaimed "happiness enthusiast," initiated a year-long project to find happiness and fulfillment, and documented her journey in "The Happiness Project."

"The Happiness Project" is divided into 12 chapters, each focusing on different areas of life that contribute to happiness, including boosting energy, cultivating good habits, finding joy in marriage, parenting with more joy, pursuing friendship, work and career, play and leisure, spiritual growth, money and material possessions, leisure and relaxation, mindfulness and self-awareness, attitudes of gratitude and generosity.
Gretchen Rubin shares personal stories and anecdotes in "The Happiness Project," making the book feel like a conversation with a friend.
Each chapter in "The Happiness Project" provides actionable advice, exercises, and ideas to help readers implement changes in their lives.

"The Happiness Project" includes scientific research and insights from various fields, such as psychology, neuroscience, and philosophy, to offer a well-rounded understanding of happiness and fulfillment.
Written in an engaging and conversational style, "The Happiness Project" is an enjoyable and entertaining read, with humor and wit.
"The Happiness Project" encourages readers to reflect on their own values, habits, and goals, promoting self-awareness and a deeper understanding of what contributes to their happiness.

By applying the principles and strategies from "The Happiness Project," readers can develop a more positive mindset, create habits and routines that support their goals, learn to prioritize and manage their time more effectively, and cultivate a growth mindset.
"The Happiness Project" can help readers stay motivated and focused on their goals, including developing better reading habits.
In "The Happiness Project," Gretchen Rubin provides actionable tips and strategies for creating lasting changes through habit formation, emphasizing small, incremental changes.
Gretchen Rubin recommends starting with tiny, manageable habits to avoid feeling overwhelmed and increase the likelihood of success.
Gretchen Rubin suggests identifying keystone habits, which have the greatest impact on one’s life, such as setting a specific time and place to read each day.
Gretchen Rubin's "Four Tendencies" framework categorizes individuals into Upholders, Obligers, Questioners, and Rebels, helping them tailor their approach to habit formation.
Gretchen Rubin advises creating an implementation intention by specifying when and where a habit will be performed, such as reading for 20 minutes each morning at 7:00 am.
Gretchen Rubin introduces the concept of habit loops consisting of a cue, routine, and reward, suggesting that identifying these components reinforces the habit, e.g., reading before bed.
Gretchen Rubin emphasizes the importance of celebrating small wins to build momentum and motivation in the process of forming new habits.
Gretchen Rubin advises against trying to change everything at once, recommending focusing on one or two habits at a time to build confidence and make sustainable changes.
tags: don't try to change everything at once
focus on one or two habits

semantic_nodes.semantic_memory: Gretchen Rubin emphasizes starting small and building incrementally to create sustainable habits. By setting achievable goals, you can build confidence and momentum, making it more likely that you'll stick to your new habit.

Identifying keystone habits, such as a specific time and place to read each day, can help establish a strong foundation for other habits to build upon, setting the stage for a consistent reading routine.

Understanding your tendency within the "Four Tendencies" framework can help tailor your approach to habit formation. For example, if you are an Obliger, you might need to create accountability with a friend or join a book club to stay motivated.
Creating an implementation intention, which involves specifying when and where you will perform a habit, can increase the likelihood of following through and help create a consistent reading routine.
Gretchen Rubin suggests being kind to yourself and not getting discouraged by setbacks when you miss a day or two. Instead, focus on getting back on track as soon as possible.
To prioritize time for reading, identify non-essential activities and replace them with reading time. Even 10-15 minutes a day can be beneficial.

If you don’t enjoy reading or struggle to find books that interest you, experiment with different genres, formats (e.g., audiobooks), and topics until you find what resonates with you. Joining a book club or asking friends for recommendations can also help.

An API, or Application Programming Interface, is a set of protocols, routines, and tools that enable different software applications to communicate with each other. It provides a standard way for software components to interact and exchange information, regardless of the programming language or operating system used.

In web development, APIs are commonly used to allow different websites or applications to interact with each other. Social media platforms often provide APIs that allow third-party applications to access their data, such as user profiles or posts, and use that data in their own applications.
APIs streamline development by providing a standard way to interact with a service or application, simplifying the development process and reducing the need for custom integration code.
APIs enable integration between different applications, allowing them to share data and functionality, leading to more innovative and useful applications.
APIs facilitate automation by allowing tasks and processes to be automated, such as automatically importing data from one application to another, or triggering actions based on certain events.
APIs improve user experience by creating more seamless and integrated user experiences, such as allowing users to log in to a third-party service using their social media account.

In the context of blockchain development, APIs are used to interact with the blockchain network and smart contracts. The Flow Access API can be used to create and manage user accounts on the Flow blockchain.
Puddle Side and Niftory are marketplaces for buying, selling, and trading non-fungible tokens (NFTs) on the Flow blockchain. They can be used to create unique items and assets for games.

Niftory can be used to design and create NFTs on the Flow blockchain. Puddle Side and Niftory can be utilized to list these NFTs for other players to buy, sell, and trade, creating a secondary market.
Integrating NFTs into a game involves using smart contracts to govern ownership and transfer of NFTs and displaying them in the game's inventory or user interface.

Players can earn cryptocurrency by participating in the mining process within an educational game on the Flow blockchain. The more mining power a player contributes, the more cryptocurrency they can earn.
The educational game can offer quests and challenges that reward players with cryptocurrency or NFTs for completion. Examples include coding challenges or answering educational questions.

Players can earn money by buying, selling, and trading NFTs on the open market within the educational game. The game encourages collecting and trading rare or valuable NFTs related to its theme or educational content.

The educational game offers staking mechanisms that allow players to earn interest on their cryptocurrency holdings. For instance, players can stake their cryptocurrency to earn a percentage of the transaction fees generated by the game.

In the context of blockchain applications, abstracting away the underlying blockchain means hiding the complexity and technical details of the blockchain from the end-users of the application. The goal is to provide users with a user-friendly and seamless experience that resembles traditional web applications, rather than a technical and complex blockchain-based interface.

When creating an onboarding experience for a blockchain-based game on the Flow blockchain, abstracting away the blockchain could involve allowing users to sign up using their email or social media accounts instead of setting up a complex wallet with public and private keys.
The game could allow users to purchase NFTs directly using a credit card or other traditional payment method instead of requiring them to purchase cryptocurrency and then use it to buy NFTs.

The game could hide the technical details of the blockchain from the user, such as transaction confirmation times, gas fees, and public and private keys, allowing users to interact with the game in a way that resembles traditional web applications.

"Evaluate's Swap Contract" refers to a smart contract developed by the blockchain company, Evaluate. The contract is designed to facilitate decentralized exchange (DEX) transactions, allowing users to swap one cryptocurrency for another without the need for an intermediary or central exchange. The smart contract uses an algorithm to determine the exchange rate between the two cryptocurrencies being swapped, and it executes the transaction automatically once certain conditions are met.

Evaluate's Swap Contract is based on the Ethereum blockchain and is written in Solidity, the programming language used for developing Ethereum smart contracts. The contract includes several key features, including a whitelist for approved users, transaction fee handling, and automatic liquidity provision. It also supports both ERC-20 tokens and Ether.

"Crypto Quest" is an educational and interactive game that teaches users about the basics of blockchain and cryptocurrency in a fun and engaging way. The game introduces new users to the world of cryptocurrency and blockchain by allowing them to explore and interact with a virtual world powered by blockchain technology.

In Crypto Quest, players navigate a virtual world where they can explore various locations and complete quests to earn rewards. The game is built on the Ethereum blockchain and is powered by smart contracts, which manage the game's mechanics and economy. Players can earn cryptocurrency tokens as rewards for completing quests and progressing through the game, which can then be used to purchase in-game items or exchanged for other cryptocurrencies.

Crypto Quest is designed to be accessible to players of all skill levels, from complete beginners to experienced blockchain enthusiasts. It uses a combination of text, audio, and visual cues to help players understand the core concepts of blockchain and cryptocurrency, such as digital wallets, mining, smart contracts, and decentralized applications (dApps).

Crypto Quest includes social features that allow players to interact with each other, join groups, and collaborate on quests. This helps to create a sense of community around the game and encourages players to learn and explore together.

At the time of my training (2021), the most popular genre of music in the world was pop music. Pop music is characterized by its catchy melodies, upbeat rhythms, and relatable lyrics, which have made it a favorite among listeners of all ages and backgrounds.

Over the last several decades, pop music has evolved significantly, incorporating elements of various other genres such as rock, R&B, hip hop, electronic dance music, reggae, and more. This fusion of styles has led to the creation of sub-genres such as pop rock, electro-pop, and synth-pop, which have become increasingly popular in recent years.

Themes of love and relationships are a staple of pop music and continue to be popular in today's music industry because they are relatable to many people and evoke strong emotions, making them appealing to a broad demographic.

Love and relationships are universal topics that transcend cultural, linguistic, and geographical barriers, making them accessible to diverse audiences. Pop music serves as a way of connecting people globally, fostering a shared experience and sense of connection.
Pop music has evolved significantly over the years and has been a dominant force in the music industry for many years, likely to remain significant for the foreseeable future.

Music genres have always been evolving, and new genres can emerge to capture a wider audience and become immensely popular. For example, Latin music saw significant growth during the late 2010s, with artists like Luis Fonsi, Daddy Yankee, and J Balvin breaking language barriers and achieving mainstream success in the United States and around the globe.

Hip hop has grown increasingly popular over the last few decades and has become one of the most dominant genres of music today, with many hip hop artists achieving both commercial success and critical acclaim.

Vegan mac and cheese is a classic comfort food. Here are three recipes: Creamy Vegan Mac and Cheese, Baked Mac and Cheese with Cashew Cream, and Vegan Mac and Cheese with Butternut Squash.
tags: Baked Mac and Cheese with Cashew Cream
Vegan Mac and Cheese with Butternut Squash

semantic_nodes.semantic_memory: Creamy Vegan Mac and Cheese requires 1 pound macaroni, 2 tablespoons olive oil, 1 onion, 2 cloves garlic, 2 cups vegan milk, 1/2 cup vegan margarine, 1 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon black pepper, 1/4 teaspoon paprika, 2 cups vegan cheddar cheese shreds, and 1/4 cup nutritional yeast. The recipe involves blending the milk mixture, sautéing onions and garlic, thickening the sauce, melting the cheese, coating the macaroni, and baking at 375°F for 20-25 minutes.

Baked Mac and Cheese with Cashew Cream uses 1 pound macaroni, 1 cup cashews, 1/2 cup water, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon black pepper, 1/4 teaspoon paprika, 2 tablespoons olive oil, 1 onion, 2 cloves garlic, 2 cups vegan cheddar cheese shreds, and 1/4 cup nutritional yeast. The recipe includes soaking cashews, blending cashew cream, sautéing onions and garlic, thickening the sauce, melting the cheese, coating the macaroni, and baking at 375°F for 20-25 minutes.

Vegan Mac and Cheese with Butternut Squash needs 1 pound macaroni, 1 medium butternut squash, 2 tablespoons olive oil, 1 onion, 2 cloves garlic, 1 cup vegan cheddar cheese shreds, 1/4 cup nutritional yeast, 1 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon black pepper, and 1/4 teaspoon paprika. The process involves cooking and pureeing butternut squash, sautéing onions and garlic, mixing ingredients in a saucepan, coating the macaroni, and baking.

Oat milk has gained popularity in recent years and is considered a fantastic alternative to soy milk.

Lentil soup is a wonderful, comforting, and nutritious meal option.
Brown or green lentils are great for soups because they hold their shape and have a mild flavor.
Before adding the lentils, sauté onions and garlic in olive oil to bring out their natural sweetness and add depth to the soup.
Lentils absorb flavors well, so it’s recommended to experiment with different spices and herbs such as cumin, coriander, paprika, and thyme.
Adding a splash of vinegar or a squeeze of fresh lemon juice can help balance the flavors and brighten the overall taste of the soup.
Cook the lentils until they’re tender but still retain some texture; overcooking can make them mushy and unappetizing.
Onions, carrots, and celery are classic aromatics that add depth and complexity to the soup; other vegetables like diced bell peppers or zucchini can also be used.
The Modern Art Museum's permanent collection features an impressive array of modern and contemporary art from the 1940s to the present, including works by Picasso, Warhol, Pollock, and Matisse.
The Contemporary Art Galleries at the Modern Art Museum showcase the museum's latest acquisitions and rotating exhibitions, often featuring cutting-edge works by living artists.
The Architecture and Design Collection at the Modern Art Museum includes iconic furniture pieces, architectural models, and design objects that showcase the evolution of modern design.

The Photography Collection at the Modern Art Museum contains works by pioneers like Ansel Adams, Diane Arbus, and Cindy Sherman, as well as contemporary photographers pushing the boundaries of the medium.
The Modern Art Museum offers special exhibitions that often feature works by renowned artists or focus on specific themes and movements. Visitors should check the museum's website for details.
The Modern Art Museum has an outdoor sculpture garden featuring large-scale installations and interactive pieces that engage with the surrounding environment.
In the context of abstract expressionism, the Modern Art Museum has notable works by Mark Rothko, known for his bold, color-field paintings that evoke emotions and contemplation.
The Modern Art Museum houses works by Jackson Pollock, a pioneer of action painting, whose works are characterized by intricate webs of drips and splatters.
The Modern Art Museum includes paintings by Willem de Kooning, a prominent abstract expressionist who blended figuration and abstraction in his works.

Mark Rothko, originally named Markus Yakovlevich Rothkowitz, was born in 1903 in Dvinsk, Russian Empire (now Daugavpils, Latvia), and immigrated to the United States in 1913 with his family, settling in Portland, Oregon.
Mark Rothko studied art at Yale University, developing an interest in the Old Masters and Impressionism before moving to New York City in the 1920s and becoming part of the city's vibrant art scene.

Mark Rothko's early work was influenced by surrealism and abstract expressionism, and he was particularly inspired by the European avant-garde, including the Bauhaus movement and the works of Pablo Picasso and Henri Matisse.
In the 1940s, Mark Rothko developed his signature style, characterized by rectangular fields of color that appear to hover and interact on the canvas, evoking profound emotional responses.
Mark Rothko believed that art should evoke feelings rather than convey specific meanings or narratives, emphasizing the importance of experiencing the artwork.

The Modern Art Museum houses several notable works by Mark Rothko, including "No. 61 (Rust and Blue)" from 1953, which features two bold, rectangular fields of color that seem to interact dynamically.
Another significant piece by Mark Rothko in the Modern Art Museum's collection is "Untitled" from 1947, showcasing his early transition to abstraction with vibrant colors and biomorphic forms.

The Modern Art Museum's building was designed by Japanese architect Tadao Ando, who is known for his minimalist and geometric style. The museum's design blends functionality, sustainability, and aesthetic appeal.
The Modern Art Museum's design incorporates an abundance of natural light through large windows and skylights, creating an immersive and contemplative environment for experiencing the art.
The Modern Art Museum's structure is made of exposed concrete and steel, providing a rugged and industrial aesthetic that contrasts with the refined, high-tech art displayed inside.
The museum's atrium includes a large, rectangular water feature that adds a serene and calming atmosphere, complemented by the gentle flow of water.
The Modern Art Museum was designed with sustainability in mind, featuring a green roof, rainwater harvesting, and energy-efficient systems, reflecting its commitment to environmental responsibility.

The Modern Art Museum's Architecture Lecture Series features renowned architects, designers, and critics sharing insights on contemporary architecture and design. These lectures are usually free and open to the public.
The Modern Art Museum occasionally offers design-focused workshops led by local designers, architects, or artists. Participants can engage with design principles, materials, and techniques.

The Modern Art Museum screens films that explore the built environment, design, and architecture as part of its Architecture and Design Film Series. Screenings are often followed by discussions or Q&A sessions with industry experts.

"Designing the Future: Architecture and Urban Planning in the 21st Century" exhibition at the Modern Art Museum explores the role of architecture and urban planning in shaping cities and communities. It features works by leading architects, designers, and urban planners, along with interactive installations and models.

User aims to create a routine to stay active and plans to do this exercise routine three times a week.
The suggested exercise routine includes a warm-up consisting of light jogging in place or jumping jacks and dynamic stretching such as leg swings, arm circles, hip circles, and torso twists.
On Mondays, the routine focuses on upper body and cardio exercises including push-ups, tricep dips, bicycle crunches, jumping jacks, and plank holds.
On Wednesdays, the routine targets lower body and core with squats, calf raises, lunges, leg raises, and Russian twists.
On Fridays, the routine covers full body and cardio exercises such as burpees, mountain climbers, dumbbell rows, wall sits, and jumping lunges.
The routine includes a cool-down phase that lasts 5-10 minutes and involves static stretching focusing on major muscle groups.

An exercise bike can be used for various workouts including interval training, endurance rides, and high-intensity interval training. Warm-up involves easy pedaling at low resistance for 5-10 minutes.

On Monday, you can perform sprint intervals by pedaling at maximum intensity for 30 seconds, followed by 30 seconds of easy pedaling. Repeat this cycle for 15-20 minutes. Alternatively, you can increase resistance to simulate hill climbing, pedal at high intensity for 1-2 minutes, then recover at low intensity for 2-3 minutes, repeating for 15-20 minutes.

On Wednesday, focus on steady state pedaling at a moderate intensity for 20-30 minutes to maintain a consistent pace. Additionally, practice cadence drills by increasing pedaling speed (RPMs) for 1-2 minutes, then recovering at low intensity for 2-3 minutes, repeating for 15-20 minutes.

On Friday, engage in Tabata intervals by pedaling at maximum intensity for 20 seconds, followed by 10 seconds of rest, repeating for 4-6 rounds, then recovering at low intensity for 2-3 minutes. Alternatively, perform proximity sprints by pedaling at maximum intensity for 10-15 seconds, then recovering at low intensity for 1-2 minutes, repeating for 15-20 minutes.
Cool down involves easy pedaling at low resistance for 5-10 minutes to gradually lower heart rate and breathing.
Adjust the resistance and intensity according to your fitness level and comfort. Track progress by monitoring distance, time, or heart rate.
Mix and match workouts or create custom routines to avoid boredom and prevent performance plateaus.

Incorporating strength training into your routine is a great way to improve overall fitness and health. You don't need a lot of equipment to get started, and you can do many effective exercises at home with minimal equipment.

Push-ups work chest, shoulders, and triceps. Squats work legs, glutes, and core. Lunges work legs, glutes, and core. Planks work core and improve posture. Pull-ups (or assisted pull-ups using a resistance band) work back and arms. Dips (using a chair or bench) work triceps and chest. Burpees work full body. Mountain climbers work legs, core, and improve cardiovascular fitness.

Resistance band exercises include bicep curls, which work biceps; tricep extensions, which work triceps; shoulder press, which works shoulders; chest press, which works chest; leg curls and leg extensions, which work legs; and chest flys, which work chest and shoulders.

Dumbbell exercises (using water bottles or cans) include bicep curls, which work biceps; tricep extensions, which work triceps; shoulder press, which works shoulders; chest press, which works chest; rows, which work back and arms; and shoulder rotations, which work shoulders.

Other exercises include wall sits, which work legs and glutes; calf raises, which work calf muscles; glute bridges, which work glutes; Russian twists, which work core and obliques; and leg raises, which work lower abs.

Start with lower reps and sets and gradually increase as you build strength. Focus on proper form and technique over the number of reps. Rest for 48-72 hours between strength training sessions for the same muscle group.
Incorporate exercises that work multiple muscle groups at once to save time and improve overall fitness. Mix and match exercises to create a routine that you enjoy and can stick to.

Proper nutrition and recovery are essential to support the exercise routine. A balanced diet, hydration, and sufficient sleep are recommended.
Wearable fitness trackers like Fitbit, Garmin, or Apple Watch can track heart rate, steps, distance, and other fitness metrics.

Fitness apps like MyFitnessPal, Strava, or Runkeeper can track workouts, distance, speed, and other metrics.

User is trying to improve their social media presence, particularly on Instagram, and has been noticing an increase in fake followers who are likely bots or fake accounts.
To deal with fake followers, users can report suspicious accounts to Instagram by tapping the three dots on the profile, selecting "Report," and choosing "Spam or Inappropriate."

Instagram provides built-in features to help identify and remove fake followers. Users can access "Insights" on their profile and review "Accounts You've Recently Followed" to look for suspicious profiles with no profile pictures or minimal content.
Users can block fake followers to prevent them from following and interacting with their content on Instagram.
Third-party apps like Followerwonk, Fake Follower Check, or Social Blade can help identify fake followers, offering free trials or limited free features.
Analyzing follower growth rates can help identify sudden spikes indicating potential influxes of fake followers. Users can track follower growth using Instagram Insights.
Verifying an Instagram account with a blue checkmark can help deter fake followers, signifying authenticity and trustworthiness.
Users should avoid engaging with suspicious accounts to prevent validating their existence and encouraging further interaction.
Focus on engagement metrics like likes, comments, and saves rather than just follower count to gauge content performance and identify genuine followers.

A social media contest can be a great way to boost engagement and attract real followers on Instagram. Contest ideas include a Photo Contest, Caption Contest, Tag-a-Friend Contest, Share-and-Win Contest, User-Generated Content Campaign, Scavenger Hunt, Q&A Contest, Referral Contest, Collaboration Contest, and Instagram Story Challenge.

In a Photo Contest, followers are asked to share a photo showcasing the product, service, or brand values, using a specific hashtag and tagging the account. Prizes can include free products, services, or gift cards.
For a Caption Contest, a compelling image or video is shared, and followers are invited to create a creative caption. The winning caption can be featured on the page along with a prize.
In a Tag-a-Friend Contest, followers are encouraged to tag friends who would appreciate the content or product. The follower with the most tags wins a prize, increasing visibility.
The Share-and-Win Contest encourages followers to share a post with friends and family. The follower who shares the post the most times wins a prize.

A User-Generated Content (UGC) Campaign asks followers to create and share content featuring the brand, which can be a video, photo, or artwork. Best submissions are featured on the page with rewards.
In a Scavenger Hunt, followers must find and collect items related to the brand or industry. The first to complete the hunt wins a prize.
The Q&A Contest involves hosting a Q&A session on Instagram Stories or Live, where followers answer questions related to the brand or industry. The most correct answers win a prize.
The Referral Contest encourages followers to refer friends to the Instagram account. The follower with the most referrals wins a prize.
A content calendar helps stay organized, consistent, and ensures content aligns with goals and audience needs.
Define content goals to identify what you want to achieve, such as increasing engagement, driving website traffic, or promoting a specific product.
Know your audience's interests, pain points, and preferences to create resonating content.
Choose content formats like photos, videos, stories, IGTV, Reels, or blog posts based on audience and goals.
Plan content themes such as "Motivation Monday" or "Tips and Tricks Tuesday" to cover recurring topics.
Schedule content consistently, whether daily, 3-4 times a week, or weekly, to maintain a steady posting schedule.
Use a calendar template or tools like Google Sheets, Trello, or Hootsuite to plan and organize content effectively.
Plan evergreen content like tutorials, tips, or behind-the-scenes content to remain relevant over time.
Leave room for spontaneity to allow for spontaneous posts, trending topics, or user-generated content.

Instagram Stories are a fantastic way to boost engagement and attract new followers. You can ask open-ended questions using the Question Sticker to encourage engagement and start conversations with your followers.
Creating polls and quizzes in Instagram Stories can be a fun way to engage followers and gather feedback or opinions on topics related to your brand.
Sharing behind-the-scenes content, like sneak peeks, bloopers, or exclusive deals, can make followers feel like they are part of a special group, increasing their loyalty.
Using the Swipe-Up feature in Instagram Stories can drive traffic to your website, blog, or other social media channels, helping to increase website traffic and attract new followers.
Organizing Instagram Stories into Highlights helps new followers discover your brand and understand what your content is about, making it easier for them to engage.
Using relevant hashtags in Instagram Stories can increase discoverability; you can use up to 10 hashtags per story, so choose them wisely to maximize impact.
Tagging other accounts in Instagram Stories can help reach new audiences and create collaborations, tapping into their follower base to attract new followers.
Creating time-sensitive content, like limited-time offers or exclusive deals, can encourage followers to engage with your stories quickly, boosting immediate interaction.
Posting Instagram Stories consistently, aiming for at least 3-5 stories per week, keeps followers engaged and interested in your content without sacrificing quality.

Reels are perfect for creating short, bite-sized content ranging from 15 seconds to 60 seconds, showcasing products, services, or behind-the-scenes moments. They allow the use of music and sound effects to enhance engagement and can be used to develop a series of videos that tell a story or highlight a specific theme. Using relevant hashtags and tagging other accounts can increase discoverability and reach new audiences.

IGTV is suitable for longer-form content, such as tutorials, behind-the-scenes footage, or exclusive interviews, with video lengths between 10 to 60 minutes. It can be used to share in-depth content that highlights expertise, products, or services and can be serialized to maintain audience interest. Adding captions and subtitles makes IGTV content more accessible and increases engagement and watch time.

Both Reels and IGTV require high-quality visuals, good lighting, and sound to stand out. Consistency in posting content is crucial for keeping the audience engaged. Engaging with the audience through comments and questions enhances interaction and makes the content more interactive and engaging through features like shopping tags, polls, and questions.

Monitoring analytics for Reels and IGTV helps identify successful strategies and areas needing improvement. Adjusting the content strategy based on analytics can optimize future posts for better performance.

Fake followers can be frustrating and affect your Instagram experience. To deal with fake followers, you can report suspicious accounts by tapping the three dots on their profile, selecting "Report," and choosing "Spam or Inappropriate." This helps Instagram's algorithms detect and remove fake accounts.

Instagram provides features like "Accounts You've Recently Followed" and "Least Interacted With" to help identify fake followers. Use these features to review your followers and remove suspicious accounts.
Monitor your follower growth rate using Instagram Insights. A sudden spike in followers may indicate fake accounts. Track your follower growth and identify suspicious patterns.

Signs of fake followers include profiles with low-quality or no profile pictures, minimal or no content, suspicious or generic usernames, and profiles that follow many accounts but have few followers.
Remove fake followers to maintain a healthy follower base. Third-party apps like Followerwonk or Social Blade can help identify and remove fake followers.
Create a whitelist of trusted followers and accounts you want to engage with. This focuses on genuine interactions and reduces the impact of fake followers.
Avoid buying followers or using services that promise to increase your follower count. This can lead to fake followers and harm your account's credibility.
Focus on engagement metrics like likes, comments, and saves instead of just follower count. This helps understand content performance and identify genuine followers.
Keep your account secure by using a strong password, enabling two-factor authentication, and regularly reviewing your account's activity.
Salads in a Jar can be prepared by layering mixed greens, veggies, proteins like chicken, tuna, or tofu, and nuts in a jar. Cover with a beeswax wrap and store in the fridge for up to 5 days.

Portion-Controlled Rice or Quinoa Bowls can be made by cooking a large batch of rice or quinoa and dividing it into individual portions. Top with roasted vegetables, grilled chicken or tofu, and a drizzle of favorite sauce. Cover with a beeswax wrap and refrigerate or freeze for up to 3 days.

Mason Jar Soups can be prepared by making a large batch of lentil, vegetable, or black bean soup and portioning it out into mason jars. Cover with a beeswax wrap and refrigerate or freeze for up to 5 days.

Grilled Chicken or Veggies can be made by grilling a large batch of chicken breasts or vegetables like broccoli, bell peppers, or zucchini. Store them in individual containers, cover with a beeswax wrap, and refrigerate for up to 3 days.
Overnight Oats can be prepared by layering rolled oats, milk, and choices of nuts, seeds, or fruit in jars or containers. Cover with a beeswax wrap and refrigerate overnight or up to 3 days.
Snack Packs can be prepared with fresh fruit, nuts, carrot sticks, and hummus. Cover with a beeswax wrap and store in the fridge for up to 3 days.
Casseroles can be prepared in large quantities and portioned out into individual containers. Cover with a beeswax wrap and refrigerate or freeze for up to 5 days.
Breakfast Burritos can be assembled by scrambling eggs, cooking sausage or bacon, and preparing tortillas. Cover individual burritos with a beeswax wrap and refrigerate or freeze for up to 3 days.

Muffin Tin Meals can be prepared by making individual portions of meals like chicken or veggie frittatas, quiches, or meatloaf in muffin tins. Cover with a beeswax wrap and refrigerate or freeze for up to 5 days.

Most certification programs in data analytics require a bachelor's degree in a quantitative field, such as Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, or Business Administration with a focus on analytics or statistics. Some programs may accept associate's degrees or equivalent work experience. Advanced certifications may require a master's degree or higher in a related field.

Entry-level certifications in data analytics typically require 0-2 years of experience in a data-related field, such as data analysis, business analysis, or data science. Mid-level certifications require 2-5 years of experience in a data-related field, with a focus on data analysis, data science, or business intelligence. Advanced certifications require 5-10 years of experience in a data-related field, with a focus on leadership, management, or specialized areas like machine learning or artificial intelligence.

Coursework for data analytics certifications generally includes foundational knowledge in statistics, mathematics, and computer programming (e.g., Python, R, SQL), data analysis and visualization tools (e.g., Excel, Tableau, Power BI), data management and storage (e.g., databases, data warehousing), machine learning and modeling (e.g., supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning), data mining and business intelligence, and communication and presentation skills.

The Certified Data Analyst (CDA) by Data Science Council of America (DASCA) requires a bachelor's degree, 2+ years of experience, and completion of a training program. The Certified Analytics Professional (CAP) by Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS) requires a bachelor's degree, 2+ years of experience, and completion of a training program. The Certified Business Analyst (CBA) by International Institute of Business Analysis (IIBA) requires a bachelor's degree, 2+ years of experience, and completion of a training program.
tags: Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)

Roasting vegetables is a fantastic way to bring out their natural sweetness and depth of flavor. Choose vegetables that will hold their shape and texture during roasting, such as sweet potatoes, Brussels sprouts, broccoli, cauliflower, carrots, red onions, and beets.
Cut the vegetables into bite-sized pieces, ensuring they are roughly the same size for even roasting. Peel the sweet potatoes and carrots, and remove stems or leaves where necessary.
Toss the vegetables with olive oil, salt, and aromatics like garlic, thyme, or rosemary. Add a pinch of black pepper and a squeeze of lemon juice for extra flavor.
Spread the vegetables in a single layer on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a silicone mat to ensure even roasting and prevent steaming.

Roast the vegetables in a preheated oven at 425°F (220°C) for 20-30 minutes, or at 400°F (200°C) for a longer period, checking for tenderness and caramelization. Shake the baking sheet after 15-20 minutes to redistribute the vegetables.

Roast harder vegetables like sweet potatoes and carrots for 20-25 minutes, while softer vegetables like broccoli and cauliflower might be done in 15-20 minutes. Ensure not to overcrowd the baking sheet to allow for even browning and caramelization.

Adding nuts or seeds to a roasted veggie quinoa salad enhances both texture and flavor. Pumpkin seeds complement the sweetness of sweet potatoes and the earthiness of Brussels sprouts due to their nutty flavor. They are also a great source of protein and magnesium.
Walnuts provide a rich, earthy flavor that pairs well with roasted vegetables like sweet potatoes and Brussels sprouts. They are a good source of omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants.
Pecans offer a buttery, nutty flavor that works well with the sweetness of sweet potatoes. They are also a good source of antioxidants and healthy fats.
Almonds can be sliced or slivered to add a nice crunch to the salad. They are a good source of vitamin E, magnesium, and healthy fats.
Cashews have a creamy, nutty flavor that pairs well with roasted vegetables. They are also a good source of magnesium, copper, and zinc.
Pistachios have a nutty, slightly sweet flavor that works well with the sweetness of sweet potatoes. They are also a good source of vitamin B6, magnesium, and potassium.
Sunflower seeds have a nutty flavor and a satisfying crunch. They are a good source of vitamin E, selenium, and magnesium.
Chia seeds have a nutty flavor and a high nutritional profile, including omega-3 fatty acids, protein, and fiber. They can add a nice texture to the salad.
Citrus vinaigrette is a fantastic choice for the roasted veggie quinoa salad. It elevates the flavors of the dish and adds a bright, refreshing touch.

For a citrus vinaigrette, use a combination of citrus juices such as lemon juice, which adds a bright, tangy flavor; orange juice, which adds a sweeter, more subtle flavor; grapefruit juice, which provides a slightly bitter, tangy flavor; and lime juice, which adds a zesty, citrusy flavor.

Balance the acidity of the citrus vinaigrette with olive oil, avocado oil, or honey/maple syrup. Olive oil and avocado oil help balance the acidity, while honey or maple syrup adds a touch of sweetness.

Add flavor to the citrus vinaigrette with aromatics such as minced garlic, which adds a pungent flavor; grated ginger, which adds a spicy, warming flavor; and chopped fresh herbs like parsley, basil, or dill, which add freshness and flavor.

Season the citrus vinaigrette with salt and pepper to bring out the flavors. Taste the vinaigrette as you go and adjust the flavors to your liking by adding more citrus juice, oil, honey, or seasonings.
Fresh herbs can add an incredible amount of flavor and freshness to citrus vinaigrette. Chopped fresh herbs can also add a pop of color and texture to quinoa salad.
Parsley is a classic choice for adding a bright, fresh flavor that pairs well with citrus. It won't overpower the other ingredients in quinoa salad.
Basil, with its sweet, slightly spicy flavor, is a great match for citrus and can add depth to vinaigrette. It's popular for many salads.
Dill's light, airy flavor pairs beautifully with citrus, especially lemon and lime, adding a fresh, summery vibe to quinoa salad.
Cilantro, with its pungent flavor, pairs well with citrus, especially lime and grapefruit, and can add a bit of heat to the vinaigrette.
Mint's cooling flavor can add a refreshing twist to citrus vinaigrette, pairing well with lemon and lime, and balancing out the acidity.
Tarragon's slightly anise-like flavor adds a unique twist to citrus vinaigrettes, pairing well with lemon and orange, and balancing the sweetness.
Choose fresh, high-quality herbs for more flavor and aroma. Chop them finely to release their oils and flavors.
Start with a small amount of herbs and taste as you go. You can always add more, but it's harder to remove excess herbs from the vinaigrette.

Serious Eats' New York-Style Pizza Dough recipe produces a crust that's crispy on the bottom and chewy on the inside. It uses bread flour, water, salt, sugar, yeast, and olive oil.

To improve your pizza crust, use high-quality ingredients including fresh yeast and unbleached, unbromated flour. Control the temperature and humidity in your kitchen to create an ideal environment for fermentation.
Neapolitan-Style Pizza Dough is a classic choice, and using type "00" flour will definitely give you a unique texture and flavor.
Caputo is a popular choice among Neapolitan pizzaiolos. Caputo's "00" flour is finely milled and has a low protein content, which makes it ideal for creating a delicate, tender crust.

All Trumps "00" Flour is a US-based brand that offers a high-quality "00" flour at a slightly lower price point than the Italian brands. It is still a great choice for making Neapolitan-style pizza.

You can find these brands at most specialty food stores, Italian markets, or online retailers like Amazon. The ideal temperature for fermenting pizza dough is between 75°F (24°C) and 78°F (25°C). This range allows the yeast to ferment the sugars in the dough at an optimal rate, producing a balanced flavor and aroma. Temperatures above 80°F (27°C) can cause the yeast to ferment too quickly, leading to off-flavors and an over-proofed dough.
In cooler environments around 68°F to 72°F (20°C to 22°C), the fermentation process takes longer, but the resulting dough has a more complex flavor profile.
The ideal humidity level for fermenting pizza dough is between 60% and 70%. This range helps prevent the dough from drying out and promotes even fermentation.
Humidity below 50% causes the dough to dry out, leading to a dense or tough crust. Humidity above 80% makes the dough too sticky or develops off-flavors.
Use a thermometer and hygrometer to monitor the temperature and humidity levels in your fermentation area. Ferment the dough in a draft-free area, away from direct sunlight and heat sources.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap or a damp towel to maintain humidity and prevent drying out. Consider using a proofing box or a temperature-controlled fermentation chamber for optimal results.
Meal prepping helps save time and energy during the week. It is recommended to start small and be flexible as a beginner.
To begin meal prepping, decide on a clear goal such as saving time, eating healthier, or reducing food waste. This goal will help maintain motivation.
Choose 3-5 meals or snacks to prep for the week based on dietary preferences, allergies, and ingredient availability. Inspiration can be found online or using apps like Plan to Eat or Yummly.
Create a grocery list based on your meal plan, aiming to buy ingredients that can be used across multiple meals to minimize waste and save money.
Purchase ingredients in bulk and consider pre-cut or pre-cooked options. Shopping at local farmers' markets or using meal kit services like Blue Apron or HelloFresh can be helpful.
Prepare ingredients in bulk such as chopping vegetables, cooking proteins, and cooking grains like rice or quinoa. This saves time when assembling meals during the week.
Invest in a set of reusable, airtight, microwave-safe, and easy-to-clean containers from stores like Target or Amazon for storing prepped meals.
Label each container with the contents, date, and reheating instructions to keep track of what you have and ensure you use the oldest items first.
Store prepped meals in the refrigerator at 40°F (4°C) or below, and reheat them to 165°F (74°C) for food safety.
Cooking scrambled eggs for meal prep involves whisking a large batch of eggs (6-12 eggs) with salt and pepper, cooking them over medium heat while stirring constantly until just set.
To ensure food safety when cooking scrambled eggs, the internal temperature should reach 160°F (71°C).
After cooking, scrambled eggs should be spread out on a plate or tray to cool quickly, preventing overcooking and making them easier to store.
Portion scrambled eggs into individual servings (1/4 cup or 1/2 cup) for easy grab-and-go in the morning.
Store portioned scrambled eggs in airtight containers such as glass or plastic containers with tight-fitting lids, or use zip-top bags or freezer bags.
Refrigerated scrambled eggs can be stored for up to 3-4 days, while frozen scrambled eggs can last up to 2-3 months.
Reheat individual portions of scrambled eggs in the microwave for 10-15 seconds, or in a pan over low heat, stirring occasionally, or in the oven at 350°F (175°C) for 5-7 minutes.
For meal prepping breakfast burritos, cook and store scrambled eggs, sausage or bacon, and cheese separately to assemble burritos in the morning.
Enhance breakfast burritos with ingredients like black beans, salsa, avocado, diced veggies such as bell peppers, onions, or mushrooms, and use whole wheat or whole grain tortillas.

Keeping kids entertained during long car rides can be a challenge, but playing engaging audiobooks or kid-friendly podcasts can transport her to a different world. Options for these can be found on services like Audible, Scribd, or your local library's digital collection.
Creating a fun playlist with her favorite songs or introducing her to your own childhood favorites can be a great way to bond and create memories. Singing along can enhance this experience.

Playing classic games like "I Spy," "20 Questions," or "Would You Rather" can keep her engaged. Additionally, creating a scavenger hunt list of items to spot on the road, like different types of cars or roadside attractions, can add excitement.
Packing travel-friendly toys, such as coloring books, sticker sheets, or small puzzles, can keep her hands busy and engaged during the car ride.
Creating bingo cards with items she might see on the road, like cows, rest stops, or billboards, can turn the journey into a game. The first one to get five in a row wins.
Preparing a special road trip snack box with surprise treats and playing guessing games about flavors or textures can keep her entertained. "Would You Rather" games with snacks can also be played.
Bringing along a tablet or portable DVD player with her favorite movies or TV shows can provide entertainment, though headphones should be used to minimize noise.
Planning regular breaks at rest stops or parks allows her to stretch her legs, get fresh air, and run around, which can help break up the drive and make the trip feel shorter.
Encouraging her to tell stories or make up her own tales and taking turns adding to the story can create a fun and interactive experience.

A Lieutenant Colonel in the armed forces has significant responsibilities in terms of strategy and tactics in combat, involving a structured decision-making process to ensure the best possible outcomes.

The decision-making process for a Lieutenant Colonel includes understanding the situation thoroughly, gathering information, identifying objectives, setting priorities, developing options, evaluating options, selecting a course of action, and executing the plan while continuously adapting.

In the initial phase of the decision-making process, the Lieutenant Colonel must understand the situation, which involves knowing the location, size, and capabilities of the enemy, the terrain, and weather conditions.
The Lieutenant Colonel gathers extensive information about the enemy and the terrain before making tactical decisions, which helps in identifying the mission objectives.

After identifying the objectives, the Lieutenant Colonel sets priorities for achieving them, develops various options to meet these objectives, evaluates each option considering potential outcomes, risks, and benefits, and selects the best course of action.
tags: selecting the best course of action

semantic_nodes.semantic_memory: A Lieutenant Colonel must balance the need to achieve military objectives with the safety and well-being of their troops. This requires an understanding of the capabilities of their own troops as well as the enemy, as well as an awareness of the terrain, weather conditions, and other factors that may impact the safety of their troops.

To achieve this balance, a Lieutenant Colonel may consider several factors. These could include careful planning and preparation, thorough risk assessments, swift and decisive actions, and effective communications with their troops. The Lieutenant Colonel must also maintain constant situational awareness and be able to adapt quickly to changing circumstances in the field.

In making decisions that affect the safety of their troops, a Lieutenant Colonel must also consider the ethical implications of their actions. They must be willing to take calculated risks to achieve their objectives, but they must also be prepared to weigh the potential costs and benefits of their actions.

In military combat, it is always the priority of every commander, including a Lieutenant Colonel, to ensure the safety and well-being of their troops. However, there may be circumstances where a difficult decision must be made to achieve a military objective even when it may mean sacrificing their troops' safety.

There have been historical instances where military commanders, such as those during World War II, had to make tough decisions involving the potential sacrifice of their troops to achieve an objective. Examples include the D-Day landings and the battle of Iwo Jima, both of which involved significant risks to involved personnel.

To access a drive in macOS, connect the drive to your Mac. Depending on the type of drive you're using (such as a USB drive, external hard drive, or network drive), the instructions for connecting it may vary.
Once the drive is connected, it should appear on your desktop. If it doesn't, you can access it through the Finder.

Open the Finder, and click on the "Go" menu in the top bar. Select "Computer" (or "Go to Folder" and type "/Volumes"). In the window that opens, you should see a list of all connected drives, including your new drive.

In macOS, you can quickly show your desktop by placing your pointer in the right corner of the Dock on the bottom of the screen and swiping to the right with three fingers. This gesture minimizes all windows to show the desktop. Repeating the gesture restores the open windows.
Alternatively, you can press the "F11" key on your keyboard to show the desktop in macOS and press it again to restore your open windows.

If you have inserted an SD card into a USB-C dongle and connected it to your Mac, you should be able to access the contents of the SD card just like you would with any other external drive. Once the dongle is connected, you should see it appear on your desktop or in the Finder under the "Devices" section. Double-click on the SD card to open it and view its contents. You can now copy, move, delete, or otherwise manage the files on the SD card just like you would with any other external drive.

The "F11" key is not present on the native Apple keyboard for Macs. Instead, users can use the "Mission Control" keyboard shortcut to quickly show the desktop by pressing "Control + F3". This action minimizes all open windows and shows the desktop. To restore the open windows, users can either click on one of the minimized windows or press "Control + F3" again.

The default keyboard shortcut to open the Terminal app in macOS is to press the "Command + Space" keys on your keyboard to open the Spotlight search, then type "Terminal" in the search field and select the Terminal app from the results, and finally press the "Enter" key or click on the Terminal app icon to launch it.
Alternatively, the Terminal app can be accessed from the Applications > Utilities folder or from Launchpad.

To create a keyboard shortcut to open the Terminal app in macOS using Automator, follow these steps: Open the Automator app found in the Applications > Automator folder or via Spotlight search. Choose "Service" as the document type. Select the "Run AppleScript" action from the "Utilities" category. Paste the script `tell application "Terminal" activate end tell` into the action. Save the service with a name like "Open Terminal." Assign a keyboard shortcut by navigating to "System Preferences" > "Keyboard" > "Shortcuts" > "Services," finding the service, clicking "Add Shortcut," and pressing the desired key combination, such as "Command + Option + T."

To delete a file in Finder on macOS, open the Finder app, navigate to the folder containing the file, select the file, and press the "Delete" key on your keyboard or right-click on the selected file(s) and choose "Move to Trash."
In Finder on macOS, you can select multiple files by holding down the "Command" key while clicking on each file.

After moving files to the Trash in Finder on macOS, you can permanently delete them by right-clicking on the Trash icon and choosing "Empty Trash" or using the "Command + Shift + Delete" keyboard shortcut.
When you delete a file in Finder on macOS, it is moved to the Trash and remains there until you choose to empty the Trash.

The default keyboard shortcut to lock the screen on a Mac running macOS is pressing "Control + Shift + Power" on your keyboard. The "Power" key is typically located in the top-right corner of the keyboard and is often represented by a small power symbol.
Alternatively, you can lock the screen by clicking on the Apple icon in the top-left corner of the screen, then choosing "Lock Screen" from the drop-down menu.
Another method to lock the screen is by using Siri to lock the screen.

To change the name of your Mac computer in macOS, click on the Apple icon in the top-left corner of the screen, choose "System Preferences," click on the "Sharing" icon, and in the Sharing preferences, you'll see a field labeled "Computer Name." Click in the field and enter the new name for your Mac. Close System Preferences.
Your Mac's new name will be reflected in the Finder, in the Network section, and in other places where your Mac's name is displayed.

To change the lock screen wallpaper on a Mac running macOS, click on the Apple icon in the top-left corner of the screen, choose "System Preferences," then click on the "Desktop & Screen Saver" icon. In the Desktop & Screen Saver preferences, select the "Desktop" tab, choose the folder containing the desired image, and select the image. Then switch to the "Screen Saver" tab, choose the folder again, and select the image. Close System Preferences.

To display the battery percentage on a MacBook without clicking on it, follow these steps: Click on the Apple icon in the top-left corner of the screen, choose "System Preferences," click on the "Battery" icon, and check the box next to "Show percentage" to display the battery percentage in the menu bar.
After completing the steps, your MacBook's battery percentage will be displayed in the menu bar, allowing you to see it without having to click on it.

The `SquareIntegration` schema includes properties `brandId`, `applicationId`, `accessToken`, and `syncEnabled`, where `syncEnabled` has a default value of `true`.

The `createSquareIntegration` method in the `squareIntegrationController` creates a new `SquareIntegration` instance with properties `brandId`, `applicationId`, `accessToken`, and `syncEnabled` obtained from the request body.

The `updateSquareIntegration` method in the `squareIntegrationController` updates an existing `SquareIntegration` instance with properties `applicationId`, `accessToken`, and `syncEnabled` obtained from the request body.
Both `createSquareIntegration` and `updateSquareIntegration` methods return JSON responses indicating success or failure, along with relevant messages and data or errors.
Client-side code interacting with the `createSquareIntegration` and `updateSquareIntegration` API endpoints needs to include the `syncEnabled` property in the request payload.

The `createSquareIntegration` function in `src/services/squareIntegrationService.js` now destructures the `syncEnabled` property from the `integration` object and adds it to the request body sent to `/square/integrations`.

The `updateSquareIntegration` function in `src/services/squareIntegrationService.js` now destructures the `syncEnabled` property from the `integration` object and adds it to the request body sent to `/square/integrations`.
